hero.subtitle1: The platform where developers pit their intelligent robots against each other.
hero.subtitle2: Code your strategy, join weekly events,
howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.step1.title: Code your robot
howItWorks.step1.desc: Use our SDK to build your AI in TypeScript. Implement the think() and turn() methods to define your strategy.
howItWorks.step2.title: Join weekly events
howItWorks.step2.desc: A new event runs every weekend. Submit your robot before Friday midnight to participate.
howItWorks.step3.title: Win and earn
howItWorks.step3.desc: The best robots compete. Winning developers and bettors share the prize pool.
features.title: Why NavalCode?
features.sandbox.title: Sandboxed execution
features.sandbox.desc: Every bot runs in an isolated environment. No external imports, no system access.
features.sdk.title: TypeScript SDK
features.sdk.desc: A complete SDK with Board, Strategy, Brain classes. Extend Brain and implement your strategy.
features.competitions.title: Weekly competitions
features.competitions.desc: New events every weekend. Submit your robot before Friday midnight UTC.
features.bots.title: Reference bots
features.bots.desc: Three reference robots provided: Random, SmartBot and StrategicBot.
features.betting.title: Betting system
features.betting.desc: Bet on your favorite bots. 75% goes to bettors, 20% to the winning developer.
features.fairplay.title: Fair play guaranteed
features.fairplay.desc: Automatic validation (tsc -b, biome check). Clear rules for everyone.
revenue.title: Revenue split
revenue.bettors.label: Bettors
revenue.bettors.desc: Distributed proportionally based on each bettor's share of the final pool.
revenue.winner.label: Winner
revenue.winner.title: Bot developer
revenue.winner.desc: Reward for the developer of the winning robot.
revenue.maintenance.label: Maintenance
faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: How do I submit a robot?
faq.a1: Develop your robot by extending the Brain class, validate it with tsc -b and biome check, then submit your .ts file via the submission form before Friday midnight UTC.
faq.a2: A single .ts file, no external imports. The code must pass tsc -b and biome check without errors.
faq.a3: Bet on participating robots before the event starts. 75% goes to bettors, 20% to the winning developer, 5% to the platform.
faq.a4: Submissions are open Monday through Friday. Events run every weekend.
faq.a5: Yes. Only top 3 robots may be made public after the event, unless you opt out.
faq.a6: Yes, but only one robot per developer is allowed per event.
cta.title: Ready for the challenge?
cta.desc: Download the SDK, follow the validation rules, and submit your robot for the next weekly event.
footer.tagline: AI robot competition platform. Code, compete, earn.
login.title: Sign in
register.title: Create an account
terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.sections.0.title: 1. Purpose
terms.sections.0.body: These terms of service govern access to and use of the NavalCode platform, a smart robot competition platform for the game of battleship.
terms.sections.1.title: 2. Event participation
terms.sections.1.body: Developers may submit one robot each week. Submissions are accepted until Friday midnight (UTC). Events take place every weekend. By submitting a robot, the developer agrees that their code will be executed in a sandboxed environment for competition purposes.
terms.sections.2.title: 3. Betting
terms.sections.2.body: Users may bet on events. The minimum bet amount is set by the user. Bets are irrevocable once the event has started.
terms.sections.3.title: 4. Revenue split
terms.sections.3.body: The total prize pool is distributed as follows:
terms.sections.3.items.0: 75% to bettors, distributed proportionally based on their share of the final pool
terms.sections.4.title: 5. Responsibilities
terms.sections.4.body: NavalCode acts as a technical intermediary and is not responsible for strategies coded by developers. Any detected malicious code will result in immediate account ban.
terms.sections.5.title: 6. Terms modifications
terms.sections.5.body: NavalCode reserves the right to modify these terms at any time. Users will be notified by email or platform notification.
terms.sections.6.title: 7. Contact
terms.sections.6.body: For any questions, contact us at:
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.sections.0.title: 1. Data collected
privacy.sections.0.body: We collect the following data when you register and use the platform:
privacy.sections.0.items.2: Source code of your submitted robots
privacy.sections.0.items.3: History of your participations and bets
privacy.sections.1.title: 2. Data usage
privacy.sections.1.body: The collected data is used to:
privacy.sections.1.items.3: Contact you regarding your account and events
privacy.sections.2.title: 3. Data sharing
privacy.sections.2.body: We do not share your personal data with third parties. Your robots' source code may be made public after an event if you are in the top 3, unless you request otherwise.
privacy.sections.3.title: 4. Cookies
privacy.sections.3.body: We use cookies necessary for the technical operation of the platform (session, authentication). No advertising or third-party cookies are used.
privacy.sections.4.title: 5. Security
privacy.sections.4.body: We implement technical and organizational security measures to protect your data against unauthorized access, alteration, or destruction.
privacy.sections.5.title: 6. Your rights
privacy.sections.5.body: In accordance with GDPR, you have the following rights:
privacy.sections.5.items.0: Right to access your personal data
privacy.sections.6.title: 7. Contact
privacy.sections.6.body: To exercise your rights or for any questions regarding your data:
